violations.rule: Atomic (minimum information principle) — front asks exactly one thing
violations.detail: Front asks two things: 'What organism originally uses this system, AND for what purpose?' Should be split into two cards.

violations.rule: Back brevity — no 'and', no dashes, no elaboration
violations.detail: Back contains a comma-separated two-part answer: 'Bacteria, for adaptive immune defense against viruses' — two distinct facts (which organism + what purpose).

violations.rule: Back brevity — ideally 1-8 words, no elaboration
violations.detail: Back is 'Small insertions or deletions (indels) that disrupt the gene' — contains a parenthetical aside '(indels)' and elaboration 'that disrupt the gene'. Should be trimmed or split.
Back is 'A double-strand break (DSB)' — contains a parenthetical. Either test the abbreviation separately or trim it.
Back is 'PAM (protospacer adjacent motif)' — contains a parenthetical expansion. The card front asks for the 'short DNA motif' so the answer is PAM; the full name should be a separate card.
Back is 'Guide RNA (gRNA)' — contains a parenthetical abbreviation. Either the card tests the full name or the abbreviation, not both.
Back is 'Non-homologous end joining (NHEJ)' — contains a parenthetical abbreviation. Split into separate cards or trim.
Back is 'Homology-directed repair (HDR)' — contains a parenthetical abbreviation. Split into separate cards or trim.
